steps.title: Use the circle form
steps.content:
A circle with center $(h,k)$ and radius $r$ has equation
$$
(x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2
$$
The right side uses the radius squared.

steps.title: Substitute the center
steps.content:
The center is $(14,19)$, so the left side is
$$
(x-14)^2+(y-19)^2
$$

steps.title: Square the radius
steps.content: The radius is $6k$, so $$ (6k)^2=36k^2 $$ The circle's equation is $$ (x-14)^2+(y-19)^2=36k^2 $$ The answer is B.
